name: Bluegrass Community
description_short: Not-for-profit credit union chartered in 1958, serving Boyd County, Kentucky residents with checking, savings, and auto loans at member-friendly rates.
description_long:
Bluegrass Community Credit Union was chartered on October 23, 1958, and received its community charter on August 23, 2004. This change expanded membership eligibility to anyone who lives, works, or worships in Boyd County, Kentucky, along with their family members. As a cooperative, not-for-profit financial institution, the credit union is governed by an elected Board of Directors comprised of volunteers from the community.

The credit union offers a range of financial products including share draft (checking) accounts with no minimum balance and no monthly fees, savings accounts, VISA ATM/Debit Cards, auto loans with pre-approval options, and online and mobile banking platforms. Members benefit from competitive loan rates and reduced fees compared to traditional banks. The institution emphasizes straightforward, easy-to-use checking accounts and promotes wealth-building through savings products. Online banking allows 24/7 account management, and the credit union has developed dedicated mobile apps for both iPhone and Android platforms.

Bluegrass distinguishes itself through its community-focused mission and member-first philosophy. The website emphasizes treating members "like family" and recognizing individuals behind each account rather than treating them as numbers. The credit union specifically markets itself to employees of the King's Daughters Health System. With a $6.00 minimum deposit to join (including a $1.00 one-time membership fee and $5.00 minimum savings balance), the barrier to entry is low, and membership eligibility is broad within their service area.

As a credit union, Bluegrass operates under NCUA insurance, providing the same deposit protection as FDIC-insured banks. The organization is fundamentally limited to serving Boyd County, Kentucky residents, which may restrict accessibility for those outside this geographic area. The website does not provide detailed information about loan terms, interest rates, or a comprehensive list of all available products, making detailed comparison shopping challenging for prospective members.
services: Share draft (checking) accounts with no monthly fees or minimum balance
Online banking with 24/7 account access and transaction capabilities
Mobile banking apps for iPhone and Android devices
Membership enrollment for Boyd County residents, workers, and those who worship in the area
Account management and transaction services through online platform
pros: No monthly fees on share draft (checking) accounts with no minimum balance requirement
Low membership barrier at just $6.00 total initial deposit ($1.00 fee + $5.00 minimum savings)
Community charter allows membership to anyone living, working, or worshiping in Boyd County, Kentucky, plus their families
24/7 online and mobile banking access available on iPhone and Android platforms
Auto loan pre-approval available online for faster lending decisions
Member-owned not-for-profit structure means profits benefit members rather than shareholders
NCUA insurance protection on deposits up to $250,000
cons: Geographic limitation: membership restricted to Boyd County, Kentucky residents only
Limited product transparency: website does not disclose specific interest rates or detailed loan terms for comparison
No information about additional services like credit cards, mortgages, or business loans that larger credit unions offer
Physical branch access appears limited to Ashland area; no multi-state branch network
best_for: Boyd County, Kentucky residents and employees seeking low-fee checking and savings accounts
King's Daughters Health System employees wanting community-focused banking with online access
First-time credit union members with minimal startup capital looking for affordable membership
Borrowers in Boyd County seeking competitive auto loan rates through a local, not-for-profit institution
diagnosis: Bluegrass Community Credit Union is best for Boyd County, Kentucky residents seeking low-cost checking and savings accounts plus auto financing through a member-owned institution. The main caveat is strict geographic membership limitation to Boyd County only, making it inaccessible for those outside this service area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Bluegrass Community — Ashland, KY Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: Bluegrass Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Ashland, KY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
